tweets.text: .@WHAAsstSecty Chung previews @SecBlinken’s trip to Costa Rica to further strengthen our relationship with the country and meet with leaders from Central America and Mexico to work on regional priorities including migration, security, prosperity, and the environment. #PuraVida https://t.co/8juZ4iSy0q

.@VP during the signing of the COVID-19 Hate Crimes Act: "Here's the truth: Racism exists in America. Xenophobia exists in America. Anti-Semitism, Islamophobia, homophobia, transphobia, it all exists. The work to address injustice wherever it exists remains the work ahead." https://t.co/vtszdFadPc
.@POTUS: We will be a strong and trusted partner for peace, progress, and security. https://t.co/yEwHFeKXEv
Today we remember and honor all service members who made the ultimate sacrifice while defending our nation. They will never be forgotten. https://t.co/NvSczXYU6c #MemorialDay https://t.co/xLY4f3u1Q2

.@POTUS has proposed a multi-year, $4 billion regional strategy to address the root causes of migration, along with a comprehensive strategy for collaborative migration management to promote safe, orderly, and humane migration throughout North and Central America. https://t.co/laf62FQNh3
.@VP Harris says U.S. diplomatic efforts to address the root causes of irregular migration in Mexico and Central America are a top priority. https://t.co/3KHV3VNVcm
Addressing the root causes of migration means breaking the cycles of desperation and providing hope at home for individuals and families in North and Central America. https://t.co/C3PNFlDrgv

The United States is committed to working with Mexico to spur economic development in Central America and southern Mexico and expand opportunities for U.S. business collaboration. https://t.co/BruOrCvM1v https://t.co/CYArz815Eg

This #MilitaryAppreciationMonth, we express our gratitude to all members of the U.S. Armed Forces and their families - both current and former - for their service, sacrifice, and contributions to our country. https://t.co/WeFn6iwINh

During #MilitaryAppreciationMonth, we celebrate members of the U.S. Armed Forces — both current and former — for their service to our country. Read the stories of three of our veteran team members in the latest DipNote below. https://t.co/JBuC3bZfTf
.@SecBlinken: "Our vision has to be bold.  We must defeat COVID-19 and prevent future pandemics." #VaccinesWork https://t.co/1cMkRzRvR6

The COVID-19 pandemic will not end at home until it ends worldwide. In response, the State Department and @USAID have been provided more than $16 billion in resources to fight this pandemic globally and prepare the world for the next one. https://t.co/lEINUEdoFh
.@SecBlinken: Diversity and inclusion makes us stronger, smarter, more creative and more innovative. https://t.co/ftXoR9esaG

Asian Americans and Pacific Islanders are integral to the Department and serve at every level. This month we celebrate the accomplishments of #AAPI employees and the diverse perspectives they bring to our work and the mission. #AAPIHM https://t.co/UCPsrtPMNj https://t.co/b1SuLbo5Hv
.@POTUS: Our greatest strength is the power of our example, not just the example of our power. https://t.co/NOmdPYfAGx

On #PKDay, we recognize the work of @StateDeptPM’s Global Peace Operations Initiative in helping partners increase their peace operations training and strengthen their rapid deployment capabilities and readiness. For more visit: https://t.co/ti4MRWncTe

On #PKDay, the U.S. honors the brave peacekeepers who have served in @UNPeacekeeping since 1948: the 4,000+ who have died in the line of duty, those who have been injured, and those who continue to risk their lives upholding the cause of peace. https://t.co/cfi6Dm3dJs

.@SecBlinken: As @POTUS has said, we believe that Palestinians and Israelis equally deserve to live safely and securely; to enjoy equal measures of freedom, opportunity, and democracy; to be treated with dignity. Read more: https://t.co/EYLiNiJaMd.

In our latest DipNote entry, an Asian-American Foreign Service Officer shares his family's history and reflects on how his heritage has shaped his philosophy and impacted his work with the Department both at home and abroad. Read his story below. #AAPIHM https://t.co/qRWTUWwgJ7

For 10 years the United States has invested in the next generation of African leaders through the Young African Leaders Initiative. YALI's first 10 years have made clear that the next 10 will be even brighter. Follow @YALINetwork to learn more about these young emerging leaders. https://t.co/XlvyLkxYyR

Throughout this Africa Week we are promoting events and initiatives that aim to increase the visibility of Africa, highlighting the continent’s diversity of culture and artistic heritage. For more, check out our website: https://t.co/LR9e3nQF1K. https://t.co/zoxMlRjGWt
.@POTUS: We are committed to stop the hatred and the bias. https://t.co/P1IB3poNtt https://t.co/CYIPwiZnx1

As part of our ongoing celebration of #AAPIHM, we are recognizing several of our @StateISN team members of Asian American and Pacific Islander descent who are working to make our world a safer place. Learn more about them in our latest DipNote below. https://t.co/lbG1ANBAG6
.@PEPFAR supports lifesaving HIV treatment for over 10 million women​, including many mothers, in 54 countries, helping them stay healthy and productive. https://t.co/d4ECM9nNhe
.@PEPFAR has enabled over 2.8 million babies to be born HIV-free to mothers living with HIV; we provide lifesaving treatment to their mothers so they can survive and thrive. https://t.co/3LRomqJEQK

PEPFAR investments also strengthen the capacity and resilience of countries to confront other diseases, including ​#COVID19, through support for nearly 300,000 health care workers, 70,000 health care sites, and 3,000 laboratories. For more visit: https://t.co/7rQXuTrTRO.
The U.S. government has invested more than $85 billion in the global AIDS response through @PEPFAR -- the most ever by one country for a single disease.

Today marks the 18th anniversary of the U.S. President Emergency Plan for AIDS Relief. Through @PEPFAR, 20 million lives have been save and millions of HIV infections have been prevented. For more visit https://t.co/nRFC6hTR4K.

Today marks the 60th Anniversary of the Department's Operations Center. Thank you to our past and present colleagues in the Operations Center for keeping watch 24/7. @SecBlinken: Ops always comes through. https://t.co/nPav6roPhB

Join @NMADMuseum today at 5 p.m. EDT for a Diplomacy After Hours event on the Department’s 24/7 crisis communication hub, the Ops Center. Learn about the importance that the center holds in protecting Americans and U.S. interests over the past 60 years. https://t.co/HRzdS0ZIEg

.@SecBlinken: “The United States is in the process of providing more than $360 million in assistance to the Palestinian people. This includes $38 million in new assistance to support humanitarian efforts in the West Bank and Gaza.” More: https://t.co/ky3MSb1iuP.

Deputy @AsstSecStateAF Akunna Cook: Our investment in public health in Africa is another example of the enduring U.S. Africa relationship, and remains a key priority for the United States government. https://t.co/KaoVigdAAO

This National Missing Children's Day, learn more about International Parental Child Abduction and @TravelGov's resources to assist parents whose children may be, or have been, abducted to and from the United States. https://t.co/SavvBAJ958

.@SecBlinken in the West Bank: “As I told Prime Minister Netanyahu and President Abbas, the United States will be moving forward with the process to reopen our consulate in Jerusalem.” https://t.co/PJVKHcTeEp

.@SecBlinken in the West Bank: “I am here to underscore the commitment of the United States to rebuilding a relationship with the Palestinian Authority and with the Palestinian people, a relationship built on mutual respect.” Remarks: https://t.co/OZaAlknp3M.

The U.S. Department of State joins the nation and the world in marking one year since George Floyd’s murder. As @SecBlinken has said, we must commit to accountability, healing, and doing the concrete work of advancing equity and justice for all. #BlackLivesMatter https://t.co/MQGly1oEYu

Today @USAmbUN Thomas-Greenfield gave remarks to @AtlanticCouncil to mark #AfricaDay. “I spent most of my career working on the African continent, and Africa is very much in my heart.” https://t.co/RSBLXJXIIT
On #AfricaDay and every day, we thank those who work tirelessly to help achieve our shared vision of a democratic, peaceful, secure, and prosperous Africa. https://t.co/6aRwDOT49w
Live now! Watch @USAmbUN Thomas-Greenfield gives remarks to the @AtlanticCouncil. https://t.co/j6AFBJKoDy

.@POTUS: "'I can’t breathe.' Those were George Floyd’s last words. We cannot let them die with him. We have to keep hearing them. We must not turn away. We cannot turn away." #BlackLivesMatter https://t.co/dxIQQRsN8W
Today at approximately 7:00 a.m. EDT, @PEPFAR will host an Africa Day program focused on global health accomplishments on the continent. Watch live at https://t.co/bEQDy0gc6l.

Today on #AfricaDay we honor the richness and diversity of African cultures and celebrate the founding of the Organization of African Unity, now known as the @_AfricanUnion. https://t.co/U4rC6iGgjM https://t.co/IKtSjEsA1D

.@POTUS: "The Republic of Korea and the United States are both nations built on innovation, and we must both meet the challenges facing us today and look to what is possible for tomorrow." Full remarks: https://t.co/9eNjBhnWZO. https://t.co/WBoPML7YvM

The U.S.-Republic of Korea Alliance is the linchpin of peace, security, and prosperity of peace in the Indo-Pacific region. Recently, @POTUS hosted President @moonriver365 for an official visit to celebrate our relationship. Read his full remarks below. https://t.co/9eNjBhFyoo

The U.S. is committed to partnering with African nations to advance democracy, economic development, trade, peace, and security in the region. Visit https://t.co/U4rC6iGgjM for more as we mark Africa Week.

.@SecBlinken: We’re reinvigorating our diplomacy, restoring respectful engagement with our partners, reaffirming our support for democracy and economic growth across the entire continent. https://t.co/lg5AxqPDrm
Last month, @SecBlinken visited with young African leaders on his virtual trip to Kenya and Nigeria. Check out our DipNote on the @YALINetwork. https://t.co/TTqoaP7vH1

FACT: The @YALINetwork is a virtual community of more than 700,000 members across Sub-Saharan Africa, providing invaluable opportunities to connect with other leaders in their community and to learn from experts in their field. 🌍 https://t.co/J298yn9FLZ

The summit aims to celebrate 10 years of YALI, strengthen African democracy and governance, educate American stakeholders, and provide a platform for YALI alumni with opportunities for skills development.

Today, the Young African Leaders Initiative will begin their 10th anniversary summit, hosted virtually. This year’s theme is “Ten Plus Ten: YALI and the United States Look Back, and Ahead, Together.” Check out @YALINetwork for more. Register here: https://t.co/rulVAyh07I. https://t.co/S4V5yDR21J

.@POTUS: Over the next six weeks, the United States of America will send 80 million doses overseas. This will be more vaccines that any country has actually shared to date, five times more than any other country. https://t.co/9jINVkKrWh

The United States is committed to leading the international COVID-19 vaccination effort and responding to the pandemic around the world. By the end of June, the U.S. will provide 80 million vaccine doses for global use. Learn more: https://t.co/8SLjtmyXIz. https://t.co/5H4IoEoFGH

.@POTUS on U.S. COVID-19 vaccination distribution efforts: We need to help fight the disease around the world to keep us safe here at home and to do the right thing of helping other people. It’s the right thing to do. It’s the smart thing to do. It’s the strong thing to do. https://t.co/BZj2O9Cffk

.@POTUS: “We want to lead the world with our values with this demonstration of our innovation, ingenuity, and the fundamental decency of the American people...We’ll share these vaccines in service of ending the pandemic everywhere.” https://t.co/PkilkwQoei https://t.co/i1JMfpGlpx

On Global Accessibility Awareness Day, the State Department reaffirms its commitment to recruiting and retaining individuals with disabilities and ensuring an accessible and inclusive Department for all. https://t.co/6rXAbCp2VB

Bees and other pollinators are under serious threat from human activities, impacting food security and biodiversity. We all have a role to play in protecting the 20,000+ wild bee species worldwide by planting locally native plants and avoiding chemical usage. #WorldBeeDay https://t.co/Pv12I9KlIw

Coordinator for Global COVID Response and Health Security Gayle Smith discussed the United States' efforts to combat the pandemic globally and @POTUS's Monday announcement to increase global vaccine sharing. More below. https://t.co/84YBTBXvn9

COVID-19 is a global challenge that requires a global response. The United States and the world will never be safe when the pandemic is afflicting our partners and allies worldwide. Our donation of 80 million vaccine doses will help save lives. https://t.co/8SLjtmyXIz https://t.co/DEtlVEYSiH

Today, we are announcing the three categories for the 2021 Secretary’s Award for Corporate Excellence. For 22 years, #SecStateACE has recognized U.S. companies that best represent American values in their operations and actions abroad. 🇺🇸 https://t.co/JijIAdGfn0

Our donation of at least 80 million COVID-19 vaccine doses will help end the pandemic everywhere. We will never be safe so long as the pandemic is raging worldwide. Learn more: https://t.co/8SLjtmQz79. https://t.co/2bkZhBVgc0

During today's briefing, @StateDeputySpox discussed @SecBlinken's ongoing travel, shared information on the $155 million in new humanitarian aid for Rohingya refugees from Burma, and took questions on the situation in Israel and the West Bank and Gaza. https://t.co/S6ijsxYWGl

The United States is committed to leading the international COVID-19 vaccination effort and responding to the pandemic around the world. By the end of June, the U.S. will provide 80 million vaccine doses for global use. Learn more: https://t.co/8SLjtmyXIz. https://t.co/GB5v4aWzHw

Arctic sea ice is a giant, white, frozen reservoir that reflects sunlight, helping keep the region cool and our global climate stable. But as sea ice melts, the water underneath absorbs rather than reflects heat, which leads to further warming. #ArcticMinisterial2021 https://t.co/MPCcCRURw1

The Arctic regulates our climate and drives ocean currents for the entire planet. The U.S. is committed to mitigating the devastating impacts of climate change and supporting resilience in the Arctic and the world. https://t.co/IN4bGz7Wad

.@POTUS on U.S. COVID-19 vaccination distribution efforts: We need to help fight the disease around the world to keep us safe here at home and to do the right thing of helping other people. It’s the right thing to do. It’s the smart thing to do. It’s the strong thing to do. https://t.co/7izeIYf5fJ

Join @ForeignAsst_gov and government champions from around the world this week for #OpenGovWeek as they host a series of events to discuss solutions, share ideas, and commit to new levels of citizen participation in government to respond and recover from the COVID-19 pandemic.

The United States welcomes the Kingdom of Denmark’s recent decision to invest $245 million in North Atlantic and Arctic defense, highlighting a mutual commitment to keeping the Arctic stable and secure. https://t.co/Jn1TQ0LEO6

The U.S. applauds Denmark’s announcement to cut CO2 emissions by 7.1 million tons or 70% by 2030. The U.S. has announced a new target to achieve a 50-52% reduction from 2005 levels in economy-wide net greenhouse gas emissions in 2030. https://t.co/XjnGSge2su https://t.co/Lxg39mzrZi
It’s #OpenGovWeek, a time for governments around the world to recommit to core values of transparency, equity, and responsibility to their citizens. Visit https://t.co/Vzkdjbz2PD for more.
The United States will work with governments around the globe and civil society advocates to advance the human rights of LGBTQI+ persons. #IDAHOTB https://t.co/JW8bAFFHYf

.@POTUS: "All human beings should be treated with respect and dignity and should be able to live without fear no matter who they are or whom they love." #IDAHOTB reminds us to treat all people with respect. Everyone has a role to play in fighting hatred and discrimination. https://t.co/RcqPbmuSp9

.@SecBlinken: Religious freedom is a key element of an open and stable society.  Without it, people aren’t able to make their fullest contribution to their country’s success.  And whenever human rights are denied, it ignites tension, it breeds division. https://t.co/8vOMUqzLyF
